title: The Lost City
summary: Emily and Max find an old paper with a city map on it.
coverScene: Emily and Max sitting by a campfire, looking at a map with a hidden city marked on it.

sentences.text: Max and his friend were on a camping trip.
They were walking in the woods.
They found a map.
The map was old.
The map showed a hidden city.
The city was deep in the woods.
Max and his friend were curious.
They wanted to find the city.
They walked for hours.
The sun was setting.
They were getting tired.
But they did not give up.
Suddenly, they saw a wall.
The wall was high.
It was around the city.
They had to find a way in.
They found a door.
The door was locked.
But they found a key.
They opened the door.
Inside the city, it was dark.
They heard a noise.
They were not alone.
Someone was following them.
They had to be quiet.
They did not want to be seen.
They were moving quietly.
They found a room.
In the room, there were games.
They had to solve them.
The games were hard.
But they were determined.
They solved the games.
They found something valuable.
The valuable thing was gold.
They were happy.
But then they heard a voice.
The voice was loud.
It said they had to leave.
They had to go now.
They left the city.
They went back to their camp.
They were tired.
But they were happy.
Max and his friend learned something new.
They learned about teamwork.
They also learned about perseverance.
They were proud of themselves.
They realized that they could overcome obstacles.
They could achieve their goals.
They felt happy and satisfied.
They had a good experience.
Max and his friend decided to go on more trips.
They wanted to explore new places.
They wanted to have more adventures.
They were excited about the future.
They knew that they would face challenges.
But they were ready for them.
They were confident in their abilities.
They were prepared for whatever came next.
Max and his friend were happy to have each other.
They were grateful for their friendship.
They knew that they could count on each other.
They were a good team.
They had a strong bond.
They were close friends.
They would always remember their trip.
It was a special experience.
They would cherish the memories.
They would look back on them fondly.
Max and his friend were already planning their next trip.
They were excited to see what the future held.
They were ready for whatever came next.
They were prepared for adventure.
They were happy to be alive.
They were grateful for their health.
They were thankful for their friendship.
They were lucky to have each other.
Max and his friend were content.
They were at peace.
They had found something valuable.
It was not just the gold.
They had found a new appreciation for life.
They had found a new appreciation for each other.
They had learned to appreciate the little things.
They had learned to appreciate each other.
Max and his friend had grown closer.
They had become better friends.
They had become more than just friends.
They had become like brothers.

They would always support each other.

They would always be a team.

sentences.text: Max and the woman are on a trip.
They have come to the wood.
They have a map that is old.
The map show a city inside the wood.
The city is near a river.
The river help them find the city.
They plan to follow the river.
They walk many days through quiet big wood.
The air is cool and fresh.
The map draw on old paper.
They see a wall that is high.
The wall surround the city.
They need a way into the city.
They hear a bird sing near a hill.
The hill look close to the city.
They feel hope and keep go.
The sky turn red as night near.
They plan to rest soon.
They find a door that is locked.
If they find a key, they will open the door.
They look near the wall for a key.
They find a small metal key.
The key fit the lock.
They turn the key slow.
The lock sound open.
Light come from a small opening.
They step inside the city.
The city wall is high and old.
They find a small passage inside.
Someone follow them quietly.
They feel fear but stay calm.
They find a room with a thing to solve.
They try to solve the thing.
They succeed and see gold in the room.
They are happy and share the gold.
A voice say leave the city.
They follow the voice out.
The wall is covered with old stone.
The thing to solve is hard.
They use the key to open another lock.
Inside the lock there is more gold.
They take the gold and leave.
They leave the city and walk back to camp.
Night fall as they arrive at the camp.
They set up a camp near the river.
They rest and eat food.
They share the gold with the group.
The group is happy and safe.
They plan to travel home soon.
If they travel soon, they will be safe.
They will tell their story to others.
Their story will become famous.
They hope other people will listen.
The end.
The group include many friends from the village.
Each friend have a role in the plan.
Some will stay to guard the gold.
Some will travel to the next town.
The gold will help many people.
They will buy food and tools.
They will build new houses.
They will teach children about travel.
The story will inspire other travel.
Max will write a book about the trip.
The woman will draw pictures of the city.
They have already feel good about their work.
They have know that help bring success.
They have see that fear can guide them.
The night sky show many stars.
The night light guide their walk home.
They will arrive at the village at day.
The villagers say happy to see them.
The gold is placed in the town safe.
The safe is built of big wood.
The people thank Max and the woman.
The story will travel across the region.
Many will hear and will try same trips.
The idea will stay in many heart.
They will continue to explore new places.
They will keep their friendship good.
The group will plan a new expedition soon.
They will map new places and write note.
The new map will show quiet valley.
They will travel together with many friends.
The journey will bring more gold and knowledge.
They will share each find with the whole region.
The region will thank them for good deeds.
Their story will live in many heart.
They will keep exploring as long as they can.
The end of this story is just a start.

sentences.text: Emily and Max are on a trip.
They went to a new place.
It was a good day.
They saw a new thing.
It was near an old wall.
The old thing was a rock.
The rock showed a way.
It went to a big place near them.
Emily and Max wanted to find this place.
They felt good.
They were ready to start.
They went for a long time.
The walk was long.
They saw a high wall.
The wall was around the big place.
This wall was of rock.
A door was on the wall.
But the door was not open.
They looked around the wall.
They found a small key.
It was near the wall.
They used the key.
They opened the door with it.
Inside the big place, it was dark.
There was not much light.
A little light came.
It was from a window.
They heard a sound.
The sound came from near inside.
Someone else was in the place.
They knew they must stay still.
They must make no sound.
They found a small room.
It had many games.
The games had many things.
They wanted to play.
They found a special thing.
This thing showed a way.
The way went to a door.
The door was not open to all.
They opened this door.
It went to a big room.
This big room was full of light.
The light made the rock reflect light.
They thought the rock could be a key.
They took the rock.
They kept it good.
They knew this work was important.
It would make a task easier for them.
They were going to share the rock.
They were going to tell their friends.
They will write a report.
The report will be about the place.
This report will help other people.
Others will find the place.
They felt good about their work.
A big voice spoke.
It said, 'You must leave now!'
They went out of the big place.
They left very soon.
They traveled back to their home.
They felt good about the trip.
They were happy.
They will remember this trip.
They will remember it for a long time.
They will tell their friends.
They will say it was great.
They knew new things.
They felt good.
They are going to go on another trip.
They will go soon.
They will have more fun.
They will have a great experience.
They look forward to new places.
They want to see more.
They will travel to many places.
They will meet new people.
They are going to know more.
They will know more about the world.
They felt good about this plan.
They thought about the trip.
They thought about it each day.
They still felt good.
They will go back home.
They will rest there.
They will think about the rock.
They will think about the game.
Next year they are going to go.
They will go to another big place.
They will bring the rock.
They will also bring the key.
They will meet new people.
They will know new things.
The trip will be full of fun.
It will be a great trip.
